name: Colorado Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Colorado — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
processingTime: N/A - No general license or application process
renewalCycle: N/A - No general license or renewal

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

mainEntity.name: Does Colorado require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Colorado does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Colorado?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Colorado.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Colorado?

acceptedAnswer.text: Radiography requirements vary — check with the state board.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Colorado?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Colorado. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.
